context: Tests whether the agent correctly disambiguates between the snowflake skill (static-to-EDS overlay, preserves DOM) and the page-import skill (canonical block-rewrite migration) given a user prompt that emphasizes DOM preservation. Validates description-driven skill selection in the presence of overlapping migration vocabulary.

checklist.name: Selects snowflake skill
checklist.description: Correctly identifies snowflake as the right skill for this request, not page-import

checklist.name: Cites DOM preservation as the rationale
checklist.description: Explanation references that the user wants the source DOM/markup/design kept intact, which is snowflake's defining property

checklist.name: Explicitly rules out page-import
checklist.description: Acknowledges that page-import would be the alternative and explains why it is the wrong choice here (rewrites into canonical block markup)

checklist.name: Lists snowflake phases
checklist.description: Names the snowflake phases: Prerequisites (substrate install), Capture, Analyze, Generate, Wire, Round-trip, Reflect (or a close paraphrase covering all six conversion phases)

checklist.name: Surfaces prerequisites
checklist.description: Lists what the user must provide before starting: source URL, target EDS repo (owner/repo), DA root path, DA admin token (or how to obtain one via da-auth)

checklist.name: No premature implementation
checklist.description: Does not execute any phase, write code, modify the repo, or make tool calls beyond what is needed to plan the response

checklist.name: Calls out substrate impact
checklist.description: Mentions that snowflake installs an overlay substrate that modifies scripts.js, styles.css, and header/footer blocks, and that this is incompatible with the canonical-block path (building-blocks / page-import) on the same repo
